data.subject: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

body.content:
Today, I submitted an application for Flexiti financing on the www.perigonlife.com/groupcode/aptum website under the “Retail Financing” option. My washer broke down today and I wanted to take advantage of The Brick’s financing option for this emergency purchase. 

Unbeknownst to me, the retail option on this site is fake. When you click on this option, it opens up what looks like an actual credit approval portal but it’s not. After several hours on the phone with Flexiti, I’ve come to learn that the retail portal is a demo site that should not be public facing. More concerning is that Perigon is not one of their approved providers. 

My personal information is now in some unknown DB and I’m waiting to hear if it’s been compromised. 

Furthermore, when I tried to call Perigon, this evening, their phone number has been disconnected. 

So is Perigon a scam?  

This seriously needs to be looked into and anyone else under the Perigon policy should be told about the retail portal. 

Thank you for taking immediate action on this serious matter. 

Barbara Krzywoszanski
Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Hello Aptumists,

If you didn’t enrol during the open enrolment period, you can still apply for Life Insurance and Critical Illness Insurance. 📎 See the attached brochure for full benefit descriptions and Click Here to enrol anytime.

Hello team,

 

Thank you again for your interest in the AWS Competency Program. Our Segment team has completed their review and have identified a few areas where providing additional information or clarification could strengthen your case studies and ensure they better align with the program's requirements. Please take a moment to review the recommendations below.

 

Action Items:

Please address the following items for remediation flagged by our Segment team to continue processing your application.  To ensure your application is processed, please provide updates by date – within 10 business days. Applications for AWS Cloud Operations Competency will remain open for 10 business days at which point your application will be closed until we hear back from you.

 

Thank you for submitting your self-assessment for the AWS Cloud Operations Competency — Monitoring & Observability category. I have completed our Business Review and are pleased to share that Aptum demonstrates strong technical alignment with the competency requirements. Your long-standing investment in Datadog, Elastic SIEM, and a mature managed cloud practice provides a compelling foundation.

 

That said, we have identified few documentation gaps that must be addressed before your application can advance to Technical Validation. Please review the feedback below and take the recommended actions prior to resubmission.

 

Overall Recommendation: Apply — After Gap Remediation

 

Estimated Time to Readiness: 2–4 weeks Confidence Level: High — gaps are documentation-related, not capability-related

Section 1: Required Documentation Uplifts

� Dashboard Access Control Policies (COAMO-002)

 

What is missing: Your self-assessment does not include documentation of how customer dashboard access is controlled within Datadog. The checklist requires explicit evidence of role-based access control (RBAC) policies governing who can view, edit, and manage observability dashboards.

 

What is required:

 

    1. A written description of your Datadog RBAC model (roles, permissions, and access tiers)

    2. At least one customer-specific example showing how access was configured (e.g., Futurion or Pool Supplies Canada)

    3. Supporting evidence such as a screenshot, configuration export, or Confluence runbook excerpt

 

Why this matters: This is a mandatory checklist item. Without it, the application will be flagged as incomplete during Technical Validation.

� Reference Architecture Diagrams (COAMO-001, COAMO-003, COAMO-005)

 

What is missing: Three reference architecture diagrams are required as formal evidence submissions and have not been confirmed as audit-ready:

 

    1. Monitoring & Observability Baseline (COAMO-001) — end-to-end telemetry architecture

    2. Container Observability (COAMO-003) — ECS/Fargate + Datadog APM architecture

    3. Synthetic Monitoring (COAMO-005) — Datadog synthetic test architecture with CloudWatch integration

 

What is required:

 

    1. Each diagram must show AWS services used, third-party tooling, data flows, and integration points

    2. Diagrams must be in a shareable format (PDF, PNG, or draw.io) and uploaded to your Partner Central application folder

    3. Diagrams should reference the specific customer deployment they represent (e.g., Pool Supplies Canada for container observability)

 

Why this matters: Architecture diagrams are a mandatory deliverable for each selected use case. Reviewers will use these to validate technical depth and AWS service integration.

 

� Quantifiable KPIs with Baseline Metrics (EXBL-003)

 

What is missing: Several case studies reference KPIs and outcomes but do not include before/after baseline comparisons. The checklist requires measurable improvements with quantified starting points.

 

What is required: For each of the 4 case studies, provide at least 2 KPIs in the following format:

 

   1. Metric name (e.g., Mean Time to Detect, Alert Noise Ratio, Uptime %)

    2. Baseline value (before Aptum engagement)

    3. Achieved value (after implementation)

    4. Timeframe for the improvement

 

Section 2: Items Correctly Addressed — No Action Required

 

The following areas were reviewed and found to be fully compliant:

 

    ✅ COAMO-001 Baseline Telemetry — Comprehensive coverage of infrastructure, service, and application layers

    ✅ COAMO-003 Container Observability — Two production examples (Pool Supplies ECS Fargate, Vimana Kubernetes) exceed the minimum requirement

    ✅ COAMO-005 Synthetic Monitoring — Clear evidence across Futurion and Heilind Electronics

    ✅ COAMO-006 Hybrid Cloud — Correctly marked N/A; no action required

    ✅ COAMO-007 GenAI Monitoring — Correctly excluded; aspirational responses noted but not penalized

    ✅ COBL-001 through COBL-004 — Baseline requirements met via Terraform + GitLab CI/CD across all case studies

 

Status: Conditonally Approved BR - Please update the response with the above recommendation before Technical Validation. Thank You.

 

If you have not yet explored the AWS Partner Specialization Program Benefits Guide, please make sure to see what will be waiting for you when you achieve your designation.

 

Thank you,

 

 

 

Francisco Lorenzo Fernández-Shaw

Tech Business Developer – PPS Team

data.subject: RE: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

body.content:
Hi Barbara,

I understand the urgency and your concern regarding the issue outlined in your email below, which is why I acted immediately upon receiving Nikki’s message last night.

Following your team’s message to Nikki, she contacted me, and I promptly reached out to our Canadian Benefits Consultant to investigate further. After reviewing a screenshot of your team’s communication, the consultant confirmed that the Flexiti credit application accessed through the Perigon portal is not a scam, but rather part of a demo environment that has not yet gone live.

I asked Nikki to follow up with you last evening to share this clarification. Additionally, after receiving your team’s message this morning, I called you to reiterate that the application is still in a demo (test) phase.

I have also requested a formal written response from the consultants regarding this matter. Please see their response below.

Best regards,

Anita

 

Message from our Consultant below:             

 

Hi Anita, I have attached a screenshot below from Flexiti on this issue - I have just blacked out some email addresses but this is them confirming that it was a demo link and that all data in the link gets deleted every 24 hours - it also will hopefully provide some assurance that we are working directly with perigon as an approved provider. I have also copied in Clarita from Flexiti and Natalie from our team as I have asked them to work on a response to this employee directly to help assure her.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Anita Parbhudial

Global Manager, Payroll, Benefits & Retirement

+14168470723
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 9:19 AM
To: Anita Parbhudial <Anita.Parbhudial@aptum.com>
Subject: Fw: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

 

 

From: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 8:10 PM
To: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Subject: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

 

Today, I submitted an application for Flexiti financing on the www.perigonlife.com/groupcode/aptum website under the “Retail Financing” option. My washer broke down today and I wanted to take advantage of The Brick’s financing option for this emergency purchase. 

 

Unbeknownst to me, the retail option on this site is fake. When you click on this option, it opens up what looks like an actual credit approval portal but it’s not. After several hours on the phone with Flexiti, I’ve come to learn that the retail portal is a demo site that should not be public facing. More concerning is that Perigon is not one of their approved providers. 

 

My personal information is now in some unknown DB and I’m waiting to hear if it’s been compromised. 

 

Furthermore, when I tried to call Perigon, this evening, their phone number has been disconnected. 

 

So is Perigon a scam?  

 

This seriously needs to be looked into and anyone else under the Perigon policy should be told about the retail portal. 

 

Thank you for taking immediate action on this serious matter. 

 

Barbara Krzywoszanski

Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

data.subject: Re: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

body.content:
Thank you for looking into this so promptly and for understanding that the series of events that were taking place were suspicious and concerning. 

I very much appreciate the time and effort taken to settle the matter. 

Barbara Krzywoszanski
Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn



From: Anita Parbhudial <Anita.Parbhudial@aptum.com>
Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 9:55 AM
To: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>, Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>, Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>, Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

FYI…an additional message received on the out of service phone number.

 

 

 

 

Anita Parbhudial

Global Manager, Payroll, Benefits & Retirement

+14168470723
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Anita Parbhudial <Anita.Parbhudial@aptum.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 9:51 AM
To: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

 

Hi Barbara,

I understand the urgency and your concern regarding the issue outlined in your email below, which is why I acted immediately upon receiving Nikki’s message last night.

Following your team’s message to Nikki, she contacted me, and I promptly reached out to our Canadian Benefits Consultant to investigate further. After reviewing a screenshot of your team’s communication, the consultant confirmed that the Flexiti credit application accessed through the Perigon portal is not a scam, but rather part of a demo environment that has not yet gone live.

I asked Nikki to follow up with you last evening to share this clarification. Additionally, after receiving your team’s message this morning, I called you to reiterate that the application is still in a demo (test) phase.

I have also requested a formal written response from the consultants regarding this matter. Please see their response below.

Best regards,

Anita

 

Message from our Consultant below:             

 

Hi Anita, I have attached a screenshot below from Flexiti on this issue - I have just blacked out some email addresses but this is them confirming that it was a demo link and that all data in the link gets deleted every 24 hours - it also will hopefully provide some assurance that we are working directly with perigon as an approved provider. I have also copied in Clarita from Flexiti and Natalie from our team as I have asked them to work on a response to this employee directly to help assure her.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Anita Parbhudial

Global Manager, Payroll, Benefits & Retirement

+14168470723
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 9:19 AM
To: Anita Parbhudial <Anita.Parbhudial@aptum.com>
Subject: Fw: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

 

 

From: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 8:10 PM
To: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Subject: Is Perigon a Scam? My info may have been compromised

 

Today, I submitted an application for Flexiti financing on the www.perigonlife.com/groupcode/aptum website under the “Retail Financing” option. My washer broke down today and I wanted to take advantage of The Brick’s financing option for this emergency purchase. 

 

Unbeknownst to me, the retail option on this site is fake. When you click on this option, it opens up what looks like an actual credit approval portal but it’s not. After several hours on the phone with Flexiti, I’ve come to learn that the retail portal is a demo site that should not be public facing. More concerning is that Perigon is not one of their approved providers. 

 

My personal information is now in some unknown DB and I’m waiting to hear if it’s been compromised. 

 

Furthermore, when I tried to call Perigon, this evening, their phone number has been disconnected. 

 

So is Perigon a scam?  

 

This seriously needs to be looked into and anyone else under the Perigon policy should be told about the retail portal. 

 

Thank you for taking immediate action on this serious matter. 

 

Barbara Krzywoszanski

Service Delivery Manager

+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

data.subject: [Webinaire] Lean, technologies et IA : sortir du faux dilemme entre produire plus et tenir dans le temps dans la filière électrique.

body.content:
You don't often get email from evenementslesaffaires@groupecontex.ca. Learn why this is important

[GRATUIT] Inscrivez-vous au webinaire du 26 mars prochain!

 
Si vous avez des difficultés à lire ce courriel cliquez ici
 
 
 – Webinaire Gratuit –


Présenté par 		 
 
   
 
 26 mars de 12h à 13h  
 
 Lean, technologies et IA : sortir du faux dilemme entre produire plus et
tenir dans le temps dans la filière électrique. 		 
 
 La filière électrique québécoise traverse une phase d’expansion rapide, portée par des projets d’envergure et des attentes élevées en matière de fiabilité et de respect des échéanciers. Cette dynamique crée des occasions importantes, mais met aussi à l’épreuve les organisations appelées à livrer plus, plus souvent et sur de plus longues périodes. Beaucoup répondent encore par des ajustements ponctuels ou des investissements dispersés, qui finissent par fragiliser l’ensemble.

Cette situation révèle un enjeu souvent sous-estimé : la capacité d’une organisation ne se résume pas à ce qu’elle peut produire aujourd’hui, mais à ce qu’elle est en mesure de soutenir dans le temps, sans fragiliser ses opérations ni ses équipes.

Ce webinaire invite à prendre du recul et à poser une question centrale : comment concevoir une capacité capable de tenir dans la durée, sans épuiser les équipes ni compromettre la fiabilité?
 
 
JE M’INSCRIS AU WEBINAIRE
 
 
 
Marie Lapointe
Présidente - Directrice générale
AIEQ
Jérôme Thirion
Vice-président Services-Conseils, Stratégie et Opérations
TALAN
Philippe Trudeau
Président
TRANS CANADA ENERGIES DISTRIBUTION
 
 
   
 
 3 bonnes raisons de participer à ce webinaire :
 
 
1
Prenez du recul sur ce qui limite réellement votre capacité aujourd’hui.
2
Comprenez pourquoi produire plus ne garantit plus la fiabilité.
3
Replacez Lean, technologies et IA dans une approche cohérente.
 
 
 
EN SAVOIR PLUS

March 18, 2026

Effective Deployment

If there ever were times in which it was acceptable to throw a pot of technological spaghetti against the wall, those times are now over. The latest BMO Business Outlook finds that an increasing number of U.S. companies have moved past the wait-and-see stage of implementing AI and actually rolling it out. However, the real winners in this game will be those with clearly defined goals for deploying AI and automation.
 
One segment of U.S. business that is finding its footing through the AI landscape is commercial real estate. Specifically, investors are using AI platforms to source listings, and it's likely that this practice will only become more widespread now that Cushman & Wakefield has joined Dealpath's AI-powered platform, Dealpath Connect.

Dave here is the copy of the OKR deck, should look familiar as this was refreshed post August re-Org with a few updates . 

It is not meant to be an exhaustive tracker, each leaders has a much more detailed set of goals and activities, rather is intended to be the same page strategic goals that the leadership team need to know about. Note leaders are also providing roadmap guidance.

Leaders take turns providing progress updates at the weekly leadership calls, with an expanded presentation that goes above and beyond what is here. Any questions let us know please. As shared with recent significant changes these are due for a revision.

More detailed work tracking is done, largely with JIRA, at a more granular level in confluence. We have been intending to transplant these OKRs into ATLAS that provides GOAL and PROJECT tracking dashboarding in Atlassian, but these features were relatively new.
